prevalence.source: European dermatology epidemiology syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some settings

vulnerablePopulations: patients with severe disease requiring systemic therapy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; severity assessment (e.g., EASI/SCORAD) in specialist care

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with access to dermatology and advanced therapies; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist dermatology pathways common; severity scoring used for systemic/biologic eligibility
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Emollients, topical anti-inflammatories, phototherapy, systemic agents, and biologics (e.g., dupilumab, JAK inhibitors) for severe disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access variability and reimbursement differences across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide access to dermatology care and advanced therapies in many systems
